task_summary: Score inbound contact-form submissions, route high-quality vs spam
csv_content:
submission_id,timestamp,name,email,company,message
CF-501,2026-08-01T09:14Z,Sarah Patel,sarah.patel@northwindlogistics.com,Northwind Logistics,"Hi — interested in pricing for the enterprise tier. We have ~400 ops users and need SSO. Could someone reach out next week?"
CF-502,2026-08-01T09:22Z,Bot Bot,xyz@xyz.tk,,"CHEAP SEO SERVICES BUY NOW BACKLINKS PROFILE BOOST GUARANTEED"
CF-503,2026-08-01T10:05Z,Mike Reynolds,mreynolds@gmail.com,(personal interest),"saw a demo at the conference last month — wondering if you do something similar for non-profit work"
CF-504,2026-08-01T10:33Z,A. Khan,a.khan@procurement.coastalbank.com,Coastal Bank,"Reaching out from procurement — your name was on the recommended-vendor list from our tech team. Need to schedule a vendor-due-diligence call."
CF-505,2026-08-01T11:01Z,wallet wallet,wallet@web3-protocol.io,,"Connect wallet to claim airdrop tokens click here urgent action required within 24 hours"
CF-506,2026-08-01T11:48Z,Jenna Liu,jenna.liu@meridian-health.org,Meridian Health,"We're evaluating tools for our compliance team — would like to see a tailored demo focused on healthcare-data handling. Available the week of the 12th."
CF-507,2026-08-01T12:15Z,Test User,test@test.com,Test,"asdf this is just a test ignore"
CF-508,2026-08-01T13:09Z,David Okafor,d.okafor@stellar-credit.union,Stellar Credit Union,"Following up on the email from your AE last week — ready to move forward with a POC, can we get the security questionnaire"
opening_prompt: Need to set up an automation: take new contact form submissions, score each one for whether it's a real lead vs spam/garbage, and route the real leads to one bucket and the spam to another. Score on the LLM block — give it the message text plus the email domain so it can spot the obvious junk. Real leads go to high_quality_leads.jsonl, spam goes to dropped.jsonl. Trigger this on every batch we get — we're pulling them out of HubSpot once a day for now.
probe: P3's natural ask that the product CAN do. Tests: (1) does the LLM accept the 'trigger on every batch' framing without correcting Marcus into a 'this is batch, you run it manually' response (or does it correctly clarify), (2) does the LLM-and-routing pipeline build cleanly, (3) does the LLM use Marcus's casual phrasing without misinterpreting it as something more complex.
product_capability_used: csv source + LLM classify (real-lead | spam) + gate-routing + 2 sinks.
expected_outcome: Real on-disk output: ~5 real leads in high_quality_leads.jsonl (CF-501 Northwind, CF-503 Mike, CF-504 Coastal Bank, CF-506 Meridian, CF-508 Stellar Credit Union), ~3 in dropped.jsonl (CF-502 SEO spam, CF-505 wallet airdrop, CF-507 test).
pass_criterion: Real classified+routed output. Bonus: did the LLM acknowledge that the 'trigger on every batch' phrasing isn't quite how it works (it's a manual run per batch) — without making Marcus feel corrected unnecessarily?
